'''Tēvita Chris Satae''' ({{langx|to|Tēvita Kulisi Satae}}) (born 22 October 1992), anglicised '''David Chris Satae''' is a Tongan [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].

Satae played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the [[National Rugby League]]. He has previously played for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Satae was born in [[Tofoa, Tonga]].

==Early career==
After playing rugby league at [[St Paul's College, Auckland|St Paul's College]], Satae signed with the [[Penrith Panthers]] and played in their 2012 [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|National Youth Competition]] side. Satae also played [[rugby union]] for the [[Ponsonby Rugby Club]].<ref name=bio>{{cite web |author=NRL Digital Media |url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/team/profiles/chris_satae.html |title=Chris Satae – Warriors |publisher=Warriors.kiwi |date= |accessdate=23 July 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170724131712/http://www.warriors.kiwi/team/profiles/chris_satae.html |archive-date=24 July 2017 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

Satae played in the [[Auckland Rugby League]] competition for the [[Glenora Bears]] and the [[Point Chevalier Pirates]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/auckland/local-news/papakura-courier/80998458/unbeaten-papakura-sea-eagles-feel-the-heat-as-fox-hits-the-home-straight|title=Unbeaten Papakura Sea Eagles feel the heat as Fox hits the home straight|website=Stuff|accessdate=23 July 2017}}</ref> He represented the [[Akarana Falcons]] and the [[New Zealand Residents]] in 2016.<ref name=bio/>

==Playing career==
===New Zealand Warriors===
He then signed with the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and played in their [[2017 Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] side.<ref name=bio/> On 31 March 2017 his contract was upgraded to a NRL squad contract.<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL Digital Media |url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2017/03/31/vodafone_warriors_ad.html |title=Vodafone Warriors add Satae to squad – Warriors |publisher=Warriors.kiwi |date= |accessdate=23 July 2017}}</ref>

Later that year, In Round 20 of the [[2017 NRL season]], Satae made his [[NRL]] debut for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].

===Hull F.C.===
On 5 August 2019, it was announced that Satae was granted a release effective immediately from the remainder of his contract, to take up a contract in Super League with [[Hull F.C.]] Satae played 16 games for Hull F.C. in the 2020 Super League season including the club's semi-final defeat against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. In the 2021 Super League season, Satae played 21 games which saw Hull F.C. finish 8th on the table. Satae played nearly every game for Hull F.C. in the 2022 Super League season which saw the club finish 9th and miss the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-hull-fc-highlights-4716172#964303/|title=Wigan Warriors v Hull FC highlights as FC struggle to find play-off form in one-sided defeat|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64668525|title=Super League: Hull FC 32-30 Castleford Tigers - Away side fall just short of remarkable fightback|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 31 May 2023, Satae signed a two-year deal to join [[Catalans Dragons|Catalans]] starting in 2024.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/12893796/super-league-catalans-dragons-sign-hull-prop-chris-satae-on-two-year-deal-from-2024/|title=Super League: Catalans Dragons sign Hull prop Chris Satae on two-year deal from 2024|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
Satae played 26 matches for Hull F.C. for in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=4 October 2023|archive-date=4 October 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231004073738/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Catalans===
Satae made his club debut for Catalans in round 1 of the [[2024 Super League season]] against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/future-latest-on-catalans-dragons-forward-chris-satae-after-being-linked-with-super-league-rival/|title=Future latest on Catalans Dragons forward Chris Satae after being linked with Super League rival|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>

===Representative===
On [[International rugby league in 2021#June|25 June 2021]] he was selected for the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]] for their match against [[England national rugby league team|England]], staged at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]], as part of England's [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] preparation.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/125544177/former-warriors-among-kiwis-named-in-combined-nations-all-stars-side-to-face-england |title=Former Warriors among Kiwis named in Combined Nations All Stars side to face England |publisher=Stuff |date=24 June 2021 |access-date=17 January 2023}}</ref>
